vulnerability.title: Disable Kernel Parameter for Sending ICMP Redirects on all IPv4 Interfaces by Default
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list

triage_decision.reason: Disabling ICMP redirects prevents the system from sending ICMP redirect messages, which could be used for network reconnaissance or attacks.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; ICMP redirect exposure; network reconnaissance

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_default_send_redirects (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_default_send_redirects). Could not verify.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to provide tool calls: review_plan then run_cmd.

We need to fix: "Disable Kernel Parameter for Sending ICMP Redirects on all IPv4 Interfaces by Default". This is a rule sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_default_send_redirects. We need to set net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects to 0. According to triage context, to make sure setting is persistent, add line to a file in directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0.

Thus remediation plan: modify file /etc/sysctl.d/ (maybe create file) with that line.

We must first review_plan: describe plan: we will create or edit file /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf? Actually any .conf file in /etc/sysctl.d/. We'll add line.

Thus review_plan output: JSON with fields: plan_description, maybe other fields? The function signature expects returning something? Likely just need to output plan_description as a string. Possibly also need to return nothing else. The spec: parameters: {"plan_description": {"type":"string", "description":"..."}} required. So we return JSON object with that field.

We'll output:

{
  "plan_description": "Create or edit file /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf to set net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects=0 for persistence."
}

Now run_cmd: we need to execute the command(s). Likely we need to ensure file exists and add line. Use sh -c "mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d && echo 'net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects=0' >> /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf". Or we could use tee -a. Simpler: run_cmd with command: "sh -c 'mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d && echo \"net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects=0\" >> /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf". That creates directory if not exists and appends line.

Alternatively we could use "bash -c 'mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d; printf \"net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects=0\\n\" >> /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf'". We'll just use simple.

Thus final answer: first review_plan JSON, then run_cmd string.

We need to output both? The instruction: "You are the Remedy agent remediating ONE OpenSCAP finding on Rocky Linux 10. ... Using the following format: ...". So we need to output the review_plan then run_cmd. Probably we output two separate blocks? The system expects we output the review_plan then the run_cmd. We'll output:

First line: the review_plan JSON. Then second line: the run_cmd string.

Thus final.
